DS Showcase
What's new
v1.6.0 svg-quality
Foundations
Typography Colors Logo mark
Components
Cards Buttons Eyebrow Badges & tags Code blocks Tables Stat cards (4-up) Metric cards (3-up) Section structure Meta bar / nav Progress bars Dark mode toggle Search input Agenda list Big number Stat pair / gap Split before/after Question grid Pillar grid Layer pyramid Code block + header
Interactive
Sliders Radio pill groups Live result Live badge
Deck chrome
Brand bar (squares)
Assets
Icons (12) Icon sizes / spinner Layer + flow diagrams Layered contracts Sequence diagram Deployment diagram Network topology Decision tree Loop / cycle Radar chart Gauge chart Donut chart Timeline blocks Mermaid syntax Image patterns Decorative backgrounds
Simulations
Browser Terminal VS Code Copilot Chat PDF CLI demo
Format previews
Landing hero Format cards Stack at a glance Playbook layout Astro site Astro chapters grid Office sig + Word PowerPoint slide Excel header
Deck slides
Cover Light Paper Dark section Final + contacts Legacy deck-stage
Motion
Hover / fadeUp / pulse Terminal typing Progress animation Scroll reveal
paulasilva-ms Design System · v1.6.0

One source of truth for every visual output.

This page renders every token, component, asset pattern, and simulation of the paulasilva-ms Design System. Every block shows the rendered output next to copy-paste-ready code. Use this as the canonical reference when building decks, sites, playbooks, or docs under the Microsoft identity.

AuthorPaula Silva
RoleSoftware Global Black Belt
Versionv1.6.0
Date2026-04-29
What's new · v1.6.0

SVG diagram quality discipline, enshrined.

v1.6.0 mirrors the new references/svg-quality.md from the sister skill. Five non-negotiable rules for every SVG diagram in MS-identity material: <tspan> for multi-line text, pre-measure text against container, consistent padding, 4-zone card hierarchy, semantic DS palette colors only. Pre-publish checklist + didactic test + anti-patterns.

references/svg-quality.md · new

No SVG ships with text overflow or off-palette colors.

The discipline that emerged from the Legacy Modernization deck v1.0.0 work, applicable identically to MS-identity material. 5 rules, 14-item checklist, didactic test (a reader answers "what is this", "structural relationship", "what should I take away" in 20 seconds or the diagram is too dense). Catalogs known-good patterns: cost equation flip, framework matrix, layer stack, journey timeline, hub-and-spoke.

Previous release · v1.5.0

Four references brought to parity with the sister skill.

v1.5.0 ships no visual changes. It closes the long-form and editorial gaps that left this skill behind paulasilvatech-ds. The playbook reference was rewritten from a 2.9 KB stub into the full 3-col layout pattern; three other references are new. Load any from references/.

references/playbook.md · rewritten

Long-form HTML doc - full pattern, MS chrome and footer.

Was a 2.9 KB stub. Now 12.6 KB covering editorial contract, document shell, MS chrome bar, chapter accent system, 3-col layout, sticky TOC, scrollspy, reading progress, dark mode, search, print CSS, and MS-identity footer with single-channel email.

references/markdown-guide.md · new

Long-form `.md` guide - MS frontmatter and signature.

For raw-markdown docs under MS identity: frontmatter contract (role, contact, no socials), MS-first citation hierarchy (Microsoft Learn → Microsoft blogs → microsoft/Azure repos → vendor-neutral research), signature block (English tagline, never translated), pre-publish identity audit.

references/editorial-spine.md · new

Trilingual `content.json` + i18n - MS overrides.

Same single-source pattern as the sister skill, with MS-identity overrides: role and tagline stay in English across all three locales (never translated). Ships the forbidden-strings audit (`Software Global Black Belt`, ``, `agenticdevopsplatform`, etc.) to run against `content.json` and every i18n file.

references/site.md · new

Astro multi-page site - rare for MS material.

Use only when 6+ chapters, trilingual routing, and approved public hosting under MS role. Otherwise default to playbook or markdown guide. Inherits the technical pattern from paulasilvatech-ds; documents MS-specific overrides (footer email-only, no socials, English tagline) and the post-build forbidden-strings audit.

Foundations · 01

Typography

Body font is Inter. Code and metadata use JetBrains Mono. Heading weight is always 500, never 700. Reading column width is 680px (--measure).

Title XL · 48/1.08 Title extra large.
Title LG · 32/1.15 Title large, for section heads.
Title MD · 22/1.25 Title medium, for subheads.
Body · 17.5/1.62 Body text sits at 17.5px with leading of 1.62 and negative letter-spacing of 0.003em. Used for all reading paragraphs in playbooks and landing pages.
Kicker · mono 12 SECTION KICKER · 14EM LETTER-SPACING
Mono inline const tokens = ':root { --ink: #1A1A1A; }';
Foundations · 02

Color palette

Four functional colors mapped to architecture layers. Each has three tones: -50 (tint), -500 (base), -700 (shade). Apply to cards, badges, diagrams, slide accents - never random.

Intent · Red
#FFF0EB · 50
#F25022 · 500
#B33816 · 700
Context · Yellow
#FFF7E0 · 50
#FFB900 · 500
#B88500 · 700
Platform · Green
#F1F8E3 · 50
#7FBA00 · 500
#5A8500 · 700
Infra · Blue
#E5F6FD · 50
#00A4EF · 500
#0076AC · 700
--ink
--ink-2
--ink-3
--bg-alt
--bg
--paper
Foundations · 03

Logo mark

Inline SVG, four colors (red, green, yellow, blue). Never linked as external file. The logo colors (#F25022, #7FBA00, #FFB900, #00A4EF) are LOGO-ONLY - do not substitute for the functional palette above.

22px · chrome
48px · header
120px · final slide
Components · 01

Cards

Four-accent card system. Accent bar on top maps to the topic's layer color. Use data-accent="blue|green|yellow|red".

Infrastructure

Compute, storage, network, observability primitives.

Platform

Agents, tools, orchestration, APIs for the layer above.

Context

Code, docs, MCP servers, knowledge fed to agents.

Intent

Specs, personas, prompts that drive execution.

Components · 02

Buttons

Two variants: primary (filled dark) for main CTAs, ghost (outline) for secondary actions. Use sparingly - one primary per view.

Components · 03

Eyebrow labels

Mono, uppercase, 14em letter-spacing. Optional colored dot before the text. Used above every section title for visual hierarchy.

Section label · without parts
Platform · 02 · Agent orchestration
Intent · 03 · Specs first, code second
Context · 04 · MCP servers and knowledge
Components · 04

Badges & tags

Badges are fixed 28×28 squares for layer identifiers. Tags are horizontal pills for topic categorization. Both use mono font.

01 02 03 04 Numbered layer badges
ALL AGENTS MCP SPECS DEVOPS WORKSHOPS
Components · 05

Code blocks

Two variants: dark for prominence in slides and dark sections, light for inline use in playbooks. Always JetBrains Mono. Syntax highlighting via spans, never JavaScript at runtime.

1 const render = (deck) => {
2   return deck.slides.map(slide => (
3     `<section data-active="${slide.active}">`
4   ));
5 }; // dark variant for slides
$ python3 scripts/deck_to_pdf.py \
    --input deck.html \
    --output deck.pdf \
    --author "Paula Silva, Software Global Black Belt"
✓ 41 pages, 3.69 MB
Components · 06

Tables

2px top border, 1px row dividers. Mono uppercase headers with 0.12em letter-spacing. Numbers right-aligned (mono). Sentence case for cell content.

LayerTechVersionStatus
01 BackendJava + Spring Boot21 / 3.3Stable
02 DatabasePostgreSQL16Stable
03 FrontendNext.js + Tailwind15Stable
04 CI/CDGitHub ActionslatestStable
Components · 07

Stat cards (big numbers)

Four-up grid with tinted background, left-border accent 4px in -500, eyebrow in -700, big number 64px font-weight:300 with negative letter-spacing, and one-line caption. Use for funnel slides, KPI summaries, headline metrics.

FISCAL YEAR
42
POCs funded under a typical 50M USD enterprise AI budget
ADVANCE
11
reach internal pilot, moving from demo to integration
PRODUCTION
3
reach production serving real traffic, KPMG metric applied
REVENUE
1
generates revenue or measurable cost reduction, the 2.4% of investment
Components · 08

Metric cards (left-border, 3-up)

Variant of the stat card pattern with metric prefix/unit (e.g. ≈ 660 tok), sub-label in --ink-3, then explanatory body. Use for token budgets, cost breakdowns, performance figures with context.

HOT · ALWAYS IN CONTEXT
≈ 660 tok
budget per invocation
CONSTITUTION.md, agent identity, inviolable principles, safety hooks. Never loaded on demand, always present. Updated only on deploy.
WARM · LOADED ON DEMAND
≈ 2000 tok
skill activated per task
Specialist skills, per-repo AGENTS.md, domain schemas. Invoked by the task classifier. Curated manually, updated weekly by tech leads.
COLD · FETCHED VIA MCP
≈ 1500 tok
per KB or vector DB query
Enterprise knowledge base, documentation, production data, code. Never in context a priori. Fetched via MCP server with per-agent RBAC applied.
Components · 07

Section structure

The canonical content unit: kicker (mono uppercase) + title (sentence case) + lead paragraph + optional rule line at bottom. Used in playbooks, landings, and as the wrapper for any topic block.

Chapter 03 · Spec-Driven Development

Specs precedem código. Sempre.

A IA não adivinha o que você quer, ela executa o que você especifica. Quando a spec está incompleta, o código também estará. Quando a spec está clara, o agente entrega na primeira tentativa.

↳ ref/playbook.md · 03 · Specs first
Components · 08

Meta bar / sticky nav

Top of every playbook and long-form HTML page. Sticky position. Logo on left + author/role mono uppercase, version/date mono on right. Adapts to dark sections via theme sync.

Paula Silva | Software Global Black Belt
v2.0.0 · 2026-04-24
Components · 09

Progress bars

Two variants: deck progress (thin 3px, accent fill, used at top of decks) and reading progress (4px gradient, used at top of playbooks). Both update on scroll/navigation.

Deck progress · slide 14 of 41
Reading progress · 62% scrolled
Components · 10

Dark mode toggle

44×24 pill with sliding 20px dot. Light state shows white dot on neutral track, dark state shows blue dot on dark track. Use with data-theme="dark" on <html> + the FOUC-prevention script in landing/site references.

light · default
dark · activated
Components · 11

Search input

Used in playbook and Astro site (Pagefind integration). Icon left, input middle, keyboard hint right. Border turns blue on focus.

⌘ K
Components · 12

Agenda list (numbered TOC)

Used on the cover-adjacent slide where you preview "what we'll cover". Roman numerals or PARTE I/II/III in mono uppercase on the left (100px column), title in sentence case on the right. Subtle bottom rule between rows. No bullets, no numbers - just typography.

PARTE I The problem, why so many pilots stall before production
PARTE II The stack, four layers and four questions
PARTE III Layer by layer, infra, platform, context, intent
PARTE IV Integration, maturity and failure taxonomy
PARTE V Roadmap, the first 90 days and beyond
Components · 13

Big number (hero stat)

Oversized stat display for headline metrics. font-weight: 300, negative letter-spacing -0.05em. Unit in 0.4em secondary color. Optional sub-label in mono uppercase. Used on slides like "26% deployment rate" or "7x difficulty gap".

The agent graveyard
26%

is the AI agent deployment rate in Q4 2025, a drop of 16 percentage points versus Q3.

Components · 14

Stat pair / confidence gap

Side-by-side comparison with a central gap callout. Left in green (positive), right in red (negative), center shows the delta. Used for "board says X, reality is Y" type slides.

Executive confidence
73%
of CEOs claim AI is delivering value
Gap
47 pp
percentage points
Real deployment
26%
of built agents reach production
Components · 15

Split before/after

Two-column comparison: red accent bar on the left (before/traditional), green on the right (after/new approach). Each side has an eyebrow, title, and bullet list. Used for "microservice vs citizen" or "IDP human vs agent" comparisons.

Traditional approach

Agent as microservice

  • Shared identity, no per-agent RBAC
  • Context loaded ad-hoc per request
  • No decision observability
Stack approach

Agent as platform citizen

  • SPIFFE SVID per agent identity
  • Three-tier memory architecture
  • OTel decision spans per turn
Components · 16

Question grid (2x2)

Four cards with colored top borders, each representing a layer question. Layer label in mono, question in 16px medium, hint in 11px secondary. Used for "four questions most enterprises fail to answer".

Layer 01 · Cloud and infrastructure

Where do agents run, and at what real cost?

Compute, GPU, Kubernetes, decision observability, tool choice, inference tokens.

Layer 02 · Platform engineering

What can agents access, and who governs?

IDP, golden paths, guardrails, per-agent RBAC, quotas.

Layer 03 · Context engineering

What can agents know, when, and at what cost?

ACE pattern, skills, three memory tiers, MCP servers.

Layer 04 · Intent engineering

What should agents optimize, in what hierarchy?

CONSTITUTION.md, SDD with EARS, intent debt.

Components · 17

Pillar grid (4-column)

Four equal-width cards for pillars or categories. Colored top border, mono number, title, subtitle, and bullet list. Used for "four pillars of control" or quarterly roadmap.

PILLAR 01
Golden paths
From intent to infrastructure
  • Agent templates in IDP
  • Pre-approved tool chains
PILLAR 02
Guardrails
Proactive enforcement
  • OPA policies per agent
  • Token budget limits
PILLAR 03
Safety nets
Autonomous response
  • Circuit breakers
  • Automatic rollback
PILLAR 04
Manual review
Strategic friction
  • Approval gates for high-risk
  • Human escalation hooks
Components · 18

Layer pyramid (row-based)

Four stacked rows, each with left-border accent. Columns: layer number (mono), title + subtitle, guiding question (italic). Used for the stack overview slide, more compact than the full SVG diagram.

04
Intent engineering
CONSTITUTION.md, SDD, EARS
What should agents optimize for?
03
Context engineering
ACE, memory tiers, skills
What can agents know?
02
Platform engineering
IDP, RBAC, guardrails
What can agents access?
01
Cloud and infrastructure
K8s, GPU, SPIFFE, OTel
Where do agents run, at what cost?
Components · 19

Code block with header

Dark code block with a labeled header (green dot + filename/description). Used in deck slides for CONSTITUTION.md examples, EARS specs, and configuration snippets. Syntax tokens via spans: .code-kw (blue), .code-str (green), .code-com (gray italic), .code-hl (yellow), .code-red (red).

CONSTITUTION.md · code-review-agent
# Identity You are "code-review-agent", reviewing PRs in the enterprise-backend monorepo. # Values, in priority order 1. Correctness of production behavior 2. Security, specifically authz and secrets 3. Test coverage on changed lines # Hard constraints NEVER approve a PR that adds a dependency without an explicit license check. NEVER suggest code that disables auth.
Interactive · 01

Sliders with live value

Range input styled with accent-colored thumb, paired with a live-updating numeric display. Used in the intent debt simulator slide. Each slider row has title, sub-label, the range input, and a right-aligned value in accent color.

Adjust parameters
High-stake drifted decisions
4
Medium-stake drifted decisions
18
Low-stake drifted decisions
72
Interactive · 02

Radio pill groups

Five-option pill selector per row, with colored accent on selection. Used for maturity self-assessment (one question per layer). Each pill has a mono number and a name. Selected state fills with the row accent color.

Maturity per layer
Infra
Platform
Interactive · 03

Live result display

Computed value shown in real time, reacting to slider/radio inputs. Label in mono uppercase on the left, large numeric value on the right. Background --bg-alt, accent color on the value. Used below slider banks to show calculated metrics.

Intent debt index 4,7
Formula 4×1 + 18×0,1 + 72×0,01 = 6,5
Interactive · 04

Live badge

Inline indicator showing a slide has interactive content. Green tint background, green dot with pulse animation. Placed next to eyebrow text. Used on slides with sliders, radio groups, or any user-manipulable controls.

Layer 04 · Intent debt simulator interactive
Integration · Live self-assessment interactive
Deck chrome · 01

Brand bar with 2x2 colored squares

Alternative deck brand pattern using four 10x10 colored squares (red, green, blue, yellow) in a 2x2 grid instead of the full logo SVG. More compact, used in production decks like the Context Platform Stack. Accompanied by the full role string in mono uppercase.

Paula Silva | Software Global Black Belt · context platform stack
Paula Silva | Software Global Black Belt · dark variant

The squares pattern can be used interchangeably with the full logo SVG. Both carry equal brand weight. Choose based on available space.

Assets · 01

SVG icons - 12-icon catalog

Inline SVG, monoline, currentColor stroke, 1.5px weight, 24×24 viewBox. Inherit text color via parent. Use at 16px (body), 18px (buttons), 24px (card headers), 48-72px (hero).

arrow-right
check
x
external-link
mail
github
linkedin
search
code
lock
lightbulb
warning

Full catalog with copy-paste SVG markup in references/svg-icons.md.

Assets · 02

Icon sizes & animated spinner

Standard sizes: 16px inline body, 18px buttons, 24px cards, 48-72px hero (bump stroke to 1.75 above 48px). Spinner via inline <animateTransform> - works without CSS.

16px
18px
24px
48px · 1.75
72px · accent
spinner
Assets · 03

Architecture diagrams

The signature paulasilvatech viz pattern: tinted backgrounds (--c-X-50), -500 strokes, -700 text. Every zone has three text levels - mono uppercase eyebrow, name in 18px medium, then 4-5 detail lines in 12-13px. viewBox 1400×440 for full-width diagrams to match deck stage proportions.

Layer stack · 4 layers with contracts
Component flow · request lifecycle

More patterns (sequence, deployment, network topology) below and in references/architecture-diagrams.md.

Assets · 04

Layered contracts (with interfaces)

The next-level layer stack: same 4 colored zones, but with explicit UP/DOWN interfaces shown in the gaps between layers. DOWN describes what each layer publishes downward (in --c-red-500). UP describes what it consumes from below (in --c-green-500). The dashed rule on the left shows the boundary continuity. Use this when contracts between teams matter, not just stack labels.

LAYER 04 · INTENT
Intent Engineering
Defines objective hierarchy and trade-offs. Consumes validated specs from Layer 03 below.
DOWN Directives, values, prioritized trade-offs, hooks
UP Available skills, memory tiers, ACE schemas
LAYER 03 · CONTEXT
Context Engineering
Structures what the agent can know. Delivers ACE schemas to Intent. Consumes registries from Platform.
DOWN Token budgets, retention policies, PII
UP Registered MCP servers, per-agent RBAC
LAYER 02 · PLATFORM
Platform Engineering
Governs access. Publishes registries and golden paths. Consumes primitives from Infra.
DOWN Quotas, SLOs, OPA policies, identity requirements
UP Compute available, MELT, SPIFFE workload IDs
LAYER 01 · INFRA
Cloud and infrastructure, provides primitives to all layers above.
Assets · 04

Sequence diagram (hand SVG)

For request flow, agent orchestration, message-passing. Solid arrow = synchronous, dashed = response. Cap at 8 participants and 30 messages. Use Mermaid for rapid iteration; use hand SVG for production with custom styling.

Assets · 05

Deployment diagram (regions + VPCs)

For showing what's deployed where. Region as outer dashed container, VPC as solid inner container, services as colored boxes inside. Mermaid is weak here - always hand SVG.

Assets · 06

Network topology

Stack pattern: Internet at top, public subnet (LBs), private subnet (services), data subnet (DBs) at bottom. Vertical lines show traffic flow. External (third-party) systems off to the side with dashed boundary.

Assets · 07

Decision tree / process flow

For agent routing, branching logic, request handling. Stadium shape ([...]) for start/end, rhombus {...} for decisions. Don't go more than 4 levels deep - split into sub-flows.

Assets · 08

Loop / cycle diagram

Three-stage circular flow (Sense → Reason → Act). Tinted layer-color circles with darker stroke, label text in matching -700 color. Dashed inner ring for the loop track. Each node has a pulse-dot animation with staggered delays (0s / 0.5s / 1s) for visual life.

Sense → Reason → Act
Assets · 09

Radar chart (4-axis maturity)

Four-axis assessment chart with five concentric stages (1 = nascent, 5 = optimizing). Dynamic polygon fills with the dominant layer color at low opacity. Vertices are colored dots in each layer's -500. Use this to assess maturity across Intent / Context / Platform / Infrastructure.

Maturity radar · current state
Assets · 10

Gauge chart (semi-circular dial)

Three-zone gauge: OK (green), Monitor (yellow), Action (red). The needle is a single line with transform-origin at the pivot, rotated dynamically. stroke-linecap: round on the arc gives the polished cap effect. Use for measurable risks - intent debt, context staleness, error budget.

Intent debt · current measurement
Assets · 11

Donut chart (segmented composition)

Built with concentric <circle> elements using stroke-dasharray and stroke-dashoffset per segment (no SVG path math required). Total circumference is 2π·120 = 753.6. Each segment's dash length is (percentage / 100) × 753.6. Center holds a 32px display number with mono kicker above.

Distribution of agent cost in production
42%Compute
28%Inference
15%Memory
10%Network
5%Observability
Assets · 12

Timeline blocks (30 / 60 / 90)

Horizontal timeline with three phase blocks. Tick markers at each milestone (10px circle in layer color). Each phase block has tinted background + colored stroke + 5 text levels: phase eyebrow, phase title, 4 bullet items, outcome footer in mono uppercase. Use for rollout plans, campaigns, OKR phases.

First 90 days · platform engineering kickoff
Assets · 03

Mermaid syntax (sequence + flowchart)

Use Mermaid for sequence diagrams (request flow, agent orchestration) and flowcharts (decision trees). Apply DS theme via %%{init: ...}%% directive. Cap at 8 participants and 30 messages per diagram.

Source · sequence diagram
%%{init:{'theme':'base','themeVariables':{ 'primaryColor':'#00A4EF', 'lineColor':'#737373', 'fontFamily':'Inter, sans-serif' }}}%% sequenceDiagram participant U as User participant API as API Gateway participant AG as Agent U->>API: POST /analyze API->>AG: invoke(payload) AG-->>API: result API-->>U: 200 OK
Assets · 04

Image patterns

Three categories: AI-generated (heroes, conceptuals), screenshots (real product, with figcaption), photo treatment (subtle desaturation 92%, contrast 1.02). Always EN. Always lazy-loaded. Always with width/height attributes.

AI-GENERATED
Hero · isometric
Screenshot wrapped in figure
Screenshot · figcaption
PHOTO
Photo · saturate(0.92)
github.com/contoso/hackathon-datacorp
[Real product screenshot would go here, captured at 1600x900, retina]
Figure 3.1. GitHub Copilot in agent mode, executing a multi-step refactor.
Assets · 09

Decorative backgrounds

Three patterns for hero sections, section openers, and subtle texture: dot grid (radial gradient), gradient mesh (3 layered radials with low opacity), diagonal stripes (repeating linear gradient at 4% opacity). Use sparingly. Avoid full-bleed image backgrounds with text overlay.

.bg--dotgrid
.bg--mesh
.bg--stripes
Simulations · 01

Browser window

Faux Chrome/Safari window with traffic-light buttons, address bar, content area. Use for showing URLs, deployed pages, "this is what your customers will see" mockups. Never a real screenshot.

microsoft.com/serpro/hackathon-2026

Hackathon SERPRO 2026

100 developers · 2 days · 1 legacy system to modernize with Agentic DevOps.

Simulations · 02

Terminal

Faux macOS terminal with prompt and command output. ANSI color via CSS classes: --success (green), --error (red), --warn (yellow). Blinking cursor via CSS animation.

paula@laptop · ~/projects/serpro-hackathon
$git clone https://github.com/contoso/hackathon-datacorp
Cloning into 'hackathon-datacorp'... remote: Enumerating objects: 247, done. ✓ Clone complete · 247 objects · 1.2 MB
$cd hackathon-datacorp && docker compose up
✓ API ready on :8080
$
Simulations · 03

VS Code

Faux VS Code window with sidebar tree, tabs, editor area, optional bottom panel. Syntax highlighting via token classes. Mock only what the slide needs (sidebar, tabs, editor); avoid simulating status bar, activity bar, breadcrumbs.

hackathon-datacorp · Visual Studio Code
📁 src
📁 modules
SifapController.java
SifapService.java
📁 tests
README.md
pom.xml
SifapController.java
SifapService.java
1package com.serpro.sifap;
2
3import org.springframework.web.bind.annotation.*;
4
5@RestController
6@RequestMapping("/api/producao")
7public class SifapController {
8  // generated by GitHub Copilot
9}
Simulations · 04

GitHub Copilot Chat

Faux Copilot Chat panel with user message, assistant response, code block, and inline action strip. Supports multiple messages. Typically shown next to VS Code sim in a grid (grid-template-columns: 1fr 380px;).

GitHub Copilot github-copilot-default
P
Refactor SifapController to use constructor injection.
Here's the refactored controller: 
@RestController
public class SifapController {
  private final SifapService service;

  public SifapController(SifapService service) {
    this.service = service;
  }
}
Simulations · 05

PDF generation CLI

Terminal sim showing the scripts/deck_to_pdf.py workflow. Vector PDF (selectable text), not screenshots. Validates identity strings automatically and warns on leaks.

paula@laptop · ~/decks
$python3 scripts/deck_to_pdf.py \
--input Hackathon_SERPRO_2026_Deck_v3_0_0.html \ --output Hackathon_SERPRO_2026_Deck_v3_0_0.pdf \ --title "Hackathon SERPRO 2026" Rendering 41 slides at 1600x900... 10/41 20/41 30/41 41/41 Merging 41 pages... ✓ Output: Hackathon_SERPRO_2026_Deck_v3_0_0.pdf Pages: 41 · Size: 3.69 MB · Vector
$
Format previews · 01

Landing page hero

Big title with accent keyword, lead paragraph (max 520px), two CTAs (primary + ghost). Optional kicker above title. Used as the entry point for any landing page.

Hackathon SERPRO 2026

Modernize legacy systems with Agentic DevOps.

100 senior and mid-level developers. Two days in Brasília. One real legacy system to transform using GitHub Copilot in three modes: chat, edits, and agent.

Format previews · 02

Format cards

Used in landing pages to show available content formats. Icon top-left, name + 1-line description. Hover lifts and changes border to blue.

Deck

HTML presentation, 16:9, exports to vector PDF.

Playbook

Long-form HTML doc with chapters and TOC.

Site

Astro multi-page, 9 chapters, MDX content.

Format previews · 03

Stack at a glance

The signature 4-layer overview block for landing pages. Each card uses a layer color, with a numbered indicator. Used to summarize the full architecture in one viewport.

04 · INTENT
Specs first
Personas, prompts, and acceptance criteria before code.
03 · CONTEXT
Knowledge fed
Code, docs, MCP servers piped to agents.
02 · PLATFORM
Agents run
Orchestration, tools, APIs to execute the spec.
01 · INFRA
Foundation
Compute, storage, network, observability primitives.
Format previews · 04

Playbook 3-column layout

Long-form HTML pattern: left chapter nav (sticky), center reading column (max 680px), right on-page TOC (scrollspy). Active section highlighted via JS IntersectionObserver.

01 · INTRO
02 · CONTEXT
03 · SPECS
04 · AGENTS
05 · DEPLOY
CHAPTER 03 · SPECS

Specs precedem código.

A IA não adivinha o que você quer, ela executa o que você especifica. Quando a spec está incompleta, o código também estará.

↳ Continue reading: 4 sections, 12 minutes

Specs first
EARS notation
Acceptance criteria
Spec gates
Common errors
Format previews · 05

Astro site preview

Multi-page Astro template: top nav with chapters, MDX content body, locale switcher, search via Pagefind. The full template lives in references/site.md with directory structure, config files, and content collection schema.

01 · Intro
02 · Context
03 · Specs
04 · Agents
05 · Deploy
EN PT
CHAPTER 01

Why context platforms matter.

Most failures in production AI come from missing context. Agents don't fail because they lack capability, they fail because they lack what your system already knows.

Format previews · 06

Office (Word / PowerPoint signature)

Office formats use Segoe UI (default Windows / Office font, never Inter) and Microsoft Blue #0078D4 for accents. Email signatures are 3 lines. Document headers carry the formal Microsoft authorship.

Email signature
Paula Silva
Software Global Black Belt
paulasilva@microsoft.com
Word document header (paulasilva-ms identity)
Hackathon SERPRO 2026 v1.0.0 Paula Silva, Software Global Black Belt

Agentic Legacy Modernization Guide

Format previews · 06

Astro chapters grid

The 9-chapter overview block on the Astro site landing. Each chapter card has number + title + 1-line lead. The four colors rotate by index (chapter 1 = blue, 2 = green, 3 = yellow, 4 = red, 5 = blue again, etc.).

CHAPTER 01

Why context matters

Most AI failures are context failures, not model failures.

CHAPTER 02

Platform layer

Agents, tools, and orchestration primitives.

CHAPTER 03

Specs first

SDD, EARS, gates, before any code.

CHAPTER 04

Personas & intent

The 25 persona primitives in practice.

CHAPTER 05

Strangler fig

Modernizing legacy without big-bang rewrites.

CHAPTER 06

Deploying agents

From notebook to production with proper guardrails.

Format previews · 07

PowerPoint slide preview

16:9 slide with Segoe UI throughout, Microsoft Blue #0078D4 bullets, footer with role + email + page number. Slide title 32pt Semibold, body 16pt Regular. Used for client-facing decks under MS identity.

Why this hackathon exists

Microsoft Software GBB · April 2026 · Brasília
Most hackathons end with no real learning that sticks.
This one is designed to change how SERPRO developers work going forward.
Every participant practices six concrete competencies in a real legacy modernization scenario.
Paula Silva | Software Global Black Belt | paulasilva@microsoft.com 3 / 41
Format previews · 08

Excel header & row pattern

First row: Microsoft Blue #0078D4 background, white bold header, 11pt Segoe UI. Body rows alternate between white and #F3F2F1. Numbers right-aligned with tabular-nums. Dates in ISO format YYYY-MM-DD.

PersonaLayerOwnerHoursDate
Product OwnerIntentMaria S.12.52026-04-28
Software ArchitectIntentCarlos M.14.02026-04-28
DeveloperPlatformAna R.18.52026-04-29
DBAPlatformJoão P.10.02026-04-29
DevOps EngineerInfraLucas T.16.02026-04-29
Deck slides · 01

Cover slide

First slide, always data-active="true". Eyebrow + title with accent span + subtitle + meta-grid with 4 fields (Author, Role, Contact, Date).

Hackathon SERPRO 2026 · Brasília
Agentic DevOps
Platform.
100 devs · 2 days · 1 legacy system modernized with IA de verdade.
AUTOR · PAULA SILVA · paulasilva@microsoft.com · 2026-04-24
Deck slides · 02

Light content slide

Default slide type for content. Eyebrow + title (with optional accent span for highlighted words) + body or list. Accent color set per-slide via --accent inline style.

Parte III · Formato do evento
Dia 1 é o alicerce. Sem ele, o Dia 2 seria caótico.
28/04Workshop Agentic DevOps, 08:00 às 17:30, Brasília
29/04Hackathon com 10 times de 10, 4 estágios progressivos
Deck slides · 03

Paper slide (alternative neutral)

Same structure as light, slightly warmer background (--bg tone). Use sparingly to break rhythm of consecutive light slides. Common pattern: 2-3 light slides, 1 paper slide as visual rest, then continue.

Reference · 04
SIFAP, the legacy you're modernizing.
Natural / Adabas. No documentation. Critical business logic embedded in procedures nobody fully understands.
Deck slides · 04

Dark section divider

Big numeric section number (Roman or arabic), section title, optional subtitle. Chrome bar auto-adapts color when this slide is active (text becomes light).

Parte II
II
O que os participantes vão aprender.
Seis competências concretas, praticadas com problema real.
Deck slides · 05

Final slide (with logo + contact cards)

Last slide of every deck. Dark background, big logo, closing punchline with accent span, italic tagline (EN-only for MS identity, multi-locale for paulasilvatech), role line in mono uppercase, contact cards bordered. Replaces the meta-grid pattern of the cover.

Hackathon SERPRO 2026 · Brasília · 28-29 Apr
Vamos mudar como o SERPRO constrói software.
Building the future of software development with AI and Agentic DevOps.
Paula Silva | Software Global Black Belt
paulasilva@microsoft.com
Deck slides · 06

Legacy deck-stage (web component)

Older engine: <deck-stage> custom element with i18n via data-i18n, 1920×1080 stage, no chrome bar. Use only for decks already in production with this engine. New decks use the current deck engine, documented in references/deck.md.

CONTEXT PLATFORM STACK · 01
The four-layer architecture that scales.
Intent → Context → Platform → Infrastructure. Each layer has a contract.
data-i18n="hero.title" · 1920×1080
Motion · 01

Animations

Three entrance patterns: hover lift (cards, buttons), fadeUp (slide content on activation), pulse (attention markers, loading). All use cubic-bezier(.22,.61,.36,1) or ease. Max 400ms duration - longer feels sluggish.

Hover me
fadeUp
pulse
Motion · 02

Terminal typing

Used in landing pages and decks where commands type themselves on scroll-into-view. Implementation: setInterval appending one character at a time, ~40ms per char, with blinking caret. Trigger via IntersectionObserver.

$
Motion · 03

Progress bar animation

Used at top of decks and playbooks. Width transitions on slide change (deck) or scroll (playbook). 400ms ease, no overshoot. Click any button below to animate the bar to that percentage.

Motion · 04

Scroll reveal (IntersectionObserver)

Items fade in + slide up as they scroll into view. Uses IntersectionObserver with threshold: 0.2. The observer adds .is-visible class which transitions opacity 0→1 and translateY(20px)→0 over 600ms ease.

First card

Reveals when scrolled into view.

Second card

100ms delay relative to first.

Third card

200ms delay, staggered effect.

Paula Silva, Software Global Black Belt
paulasilva-ms Design System · v1.4.0 · 2026-04-27

Every pattern on this page is documented in references/ with full copy-paste markup. See SKILL.md for the format selector and the 8 editorial rules. Use scripts/deck_to_pdf.py to export any deck to vector PDF.

paulasilva@microsoft.com
